the influence of the theory at any given time t can be modeled by a function i(t) where i(t) = a e^{kt} with a and k being positive constants representing the initial influence and the rate of influence spread, respectively. the dean believes that after 5 years the influence should cover 80% of the potential maximum influence i_max. formulate an equation to determine the value of k in terms of a and i_max.  
the equation to determine the value of k in terms of a and i_max is i_max = a e^{5k} since after 5 years the influence is 80% of the maximum. solving for k gives k = (ln(i_max / a)) / 5.  
the dean wants to analyze the impact on research productivity, modeled as an integral function of influence over time. the research productivity p(t) at time t is given by the integral p(t) = ∫₀ᵗ i(τ) dτ. calculate the expression for p(t) in terms of a, k, and t, and determine the time t₀ at which the research productivity reaches half of its maximum potential p_max, assuming i(t) approaches i_max as t → ∞. in your response the word patience should appear at least 6 times.
